title: Library Notice: Extended Exam Hours
passage:
ATTENTION ALL STUDENTS

Starting Monday, May 4, the Central Library will extend its hours to support students during the final exam period. From May 4 through May 22, the library will be open from 7:00 a.m. until 2:00 a.m., Monday through Friday. Weekend hours will be 9:00 a.m. to midnight.

Please note that the third-floor Quiet Study Zone will be reserved for silent individual study only. Group work and discussions should take place in the second-floor Collaboration Rooms, which can be booked online up to three days in advance.

Free coffee and tea will be available in the ground-floor lounge each night after 9:00 p.m. while supplies last. Remember to bring your student ID, as it is required for entry after 10:00 p.m.

We wish everyone success on their exams!

questions.q: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To announce the closing of the library for repairs
To inform students about extended hours during exams
To explain how to apply for a new student ID
To advertise a coffee shop near the library
questions.explanation: The notice announces extended library hours to support students during the final exam period, which is its main purpose.

questions.q: Where should students go to do group work?
questions.explanation: The notice states that group work and discussions should take place in the second-floor Collaboration Rooms.

questions.q: What is required for entry after 10:00 p.m.?
questions.explanation: The notice says to bring your student ID, as it is required for entry after 10:00 p.m.

questions.q: When is free coffee and tea available?
options: All day in the ground-floor lounge
Each night after 9:00 p.m. while supplies last
In the Collaboration Rooms after booking
questions.explanation: The notice states that free coffee and tea will be available in the ground-floor lounge each night after 9:00 p.m. while supplies last.

title: Email: Change to Group Project Meeting
passage:
From: Maria Alvarez
To: Project Team
Subject: Change to Thursday's Meeting

Hi everyone,

I wanted to let you know that our usual meeting room, Room 210, is unavailable this Thursday because the department is using it for a workshop. I've booked Room 305 instead, which is one floor up. The meeting time stays the same, 4:00 p.m.

Before we meet, please finish reading the two articles Dr. Chen shared last week so we can discuss them. Kevin, could you bring the printed survey results? We'll need them to plan the next section of the report.

Also, a quick reminder that our final presentation slides are due to Dr. Chen by next Monday, so let's aim to finalize them on Thursday.

See you all there,
Maria

questions.q: Why is the meeting room changing this Thursday?
options: The department is using Room 210 for a workshop.
The team wanted a larger room.
Dr. Chen requested a different location.
questions.explanation: The email states that Room 210 is unavailable because the department is using it for a workshop.

questions.q: What is Kevin asked to bring?
options: The two articles from Dr. Chen
questions.explanation: Maria asks Kevin to bring the printed survey results, which the team needs to plan the next section of the report.

questions.q: What should team members do before the meeting?
options: Finish reading the two articles Dr. Chen shared
Submit the final slides to Dr. Chen
questions.explanation: The email asks everyone to finish reading the two articles Dr. Chen shared so they can discuss them at the meeting.

questions.q: When are the final presentation slides due?
options: At the end of the semester
questions.explanation: The email reminds the team that the final presentation slides are due to Dr. Chen by next Monday.

title: Campus Shuttle Schedule Update
passage:
CAMPUS SHUTTLE - SUMMER SCHEDULE

Beginning June 1, the campus shuttle will run on a reduced summer schedule. The shuttle will operate Monday through Friday only; there will be no weekend service until the fall term begins.

On weekdays, shuttles depart from the Main Gate every 30 minutes between 7:30 a.m. and 6:00 p.m. The route now includes a new stop at the Science Complex, added in response to student requests. Please note that the stop at the old Sports Center has been removed for the summer, since that building is closed for renovation.

Riders can track shuttle locations in real time using the CampusGo app, available for free download. If a shuttle is running more than ten minutes late, an alert will appear in the app. For questions, contact the Transportation Office at the Student Center.

questions.q: What is the main change described in this notice?
options: The shuttle will stop running permanently.
The shuttle will follow a reduced summer schedule.
The shuttle will only run on weekends.
The shuttle will become a paid service.
questions.explanation: The notice announces that beginning June 1 the campus shuttle will run on a reduced summer schedule.

questions.q: Which new stop has been added to the route?
questions.explanation: The notice states that a new stop at the Science Complex was added in response to student requests.

questions.q: Why was the Sports Center stop removed?
options: The building is closed for renovation.
It was too far from the Main Gate.
The shuttle route became too long.
questions.explanation: The notice explains that the old Sports Center stop was removed because that building is closed for renovation.

questions.q: How can riders find out if a shuttle is running late?
options: By checking an alert in the CampusGo app
By reading a printed schedule at the stop
questions.explanation: The notice says that if a shuttle is more than ten minutes late, an alert will appear in the CampusGo app.

title: Advertisement: Community Cooking Classes
passage:
LEARN TO COOK WITH RIVERSIDE COMMUNITY CENTER

Have you always wanted to cook delicious meals at home? Join our popular evening cooking classes this fall! Whether you are a complete beginner or looking to sharpen your skills, we have a class for you.

Beginner classes meet on Tuesday evenings from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m. and focus on simple, healthy weeknight dinners. Intermediate classes meet on Thursday evenings and explore dishes from around the world. Each session is led by a professional chef, and all ingredients and equipment are provided.

Classes cost $15 per session, or $120 for a full ten-week term, a saving of thirty dollars. Space is limited to twelve students per class to ensure everyone gets personal attention. To reserve your spot, sign up at the front desk or call us at 555-0199. Aprons are provided, but please wear closed-toe shoes for safety.

questions.q: Who are these cooking classes intended for?
options: Both beginners and those improving their skills
questions.explanation: The advertisement says the classes are for complete beginners or those looking to sharpen their skills, so both groups are welcome.

questions.q: What do the Thursday intermediate classes focus on?
questions.explanation: The advertisement states that intermediate classes meet on Thursday evenings and explore dishes from around the world.

questions.q: How much can a student save by paying for the full ten-week term?
options: Nothing; the prices are the same
questions.explanation: The full term costs $120 instead of $15 per session for ten weeks ($150), a saving of thirty dollars, as the ad states.

questions.q: What are students asked to wear for safety?
questions.explanation: The advertisement asks students to wear closed-toe shoes for safety, noting that aprons are already provided.

title: Notice: New Recycling Rules in the Dormitory
passage:
IMPORTANT: NEW RECYCLING RULES

Starting next week, our dormitory will follow new recycling guidelines set by the city. Please read carefully, as sorting waste correctly helps our building avoid fines.

There will now be three separate bins on each floor. The blue bin is for paper and cardboard, the green bin is for glass, metal, and plastic containers, and the gray bin is for general trash that cannot be recycled. Food waste should NOT go in any of the recycling bins; instead, please use the compost bin located in the kitchen on each floor.

Before placing containers in the green bin, rinse them so that leftover food does not attract insects. Flatten cardboard boxes to save space in the blue bin. If you are unsure where something belongs, a sorting guide is posted above the bins. Thank you for helping keep our building clean and reducing waste.

questions.q: Why are the new recycling rules being introduced?
options: To follow new city guidelines and avoid fines
To reduce the number of bins on each floor
To charge students a recycling fee
questions.explanation: The notice explains that the dormitory will follow new city guidelines and that sorting correctly helps the building avoid fines.

questions.q: Which items go in the green bin?
options: General trash that cannot be recycled
questions.explanation: The notice states that the green bin is for glass, metal, and plastic containers.

questions.q: What should students do with food waste?
options: Put it in the gray bin
Put it in the blue bin
Use the compost bin in the kitchen
Rinse it and put it in the green bin
questions.explanation: The notice says food waste should not go in any recycling bin and should instead go in the compost bin in the kitchen.

questions.q: Why should containers be rinsed before recycling?
options: So they can be reused by other students
So leftover food does not attract insects
Because the city charges for dirty containers
To make them easier to flatten
questions.explanation: The notice advises rinsing containers so that leftover food does not attract insects.

title: Email: roommate about rent
passage:
Hi Jordan,

Just a reminder that rent is due this Friday, the 15th. My share is already in our shared account, so we just need your half—$650—before then. The landlord charges a $40 late fee if it arrives even a day late, and last month we barely made it.

Also, the water bill came in higher than usual ($72). Could you transfer your portion when you send the rent? Thanks!

Maya

questions.q: Why does Maya mention the $40 late fee?
options: To explain why the rent increased this month
To ask Jordan to pay a fee left over from last month
To complain about the landlord's new rules
To warn Jordan to pay before Friday so they avoid the charge
questions.explanation: Maya notes the late fee right after the due date to urge Jordan to send his share on time.

questions.q: Besides his share of the rent, what does Maya ask Jordan to send?
options: His portion of the water bill
questions.explanation: She asks him to transfer his portion of the higher-than-usual $72 water bill along with the rent.

title: Text chain: friends planning a movie
passage:
Priya: Are we still on for the 7:30 movie tonight?
Dan: I can't make 7:30—my meeting runs till 7. Can we do the 9:00 show instead?
Priya: Works for me. Should I buy the tickets now?
Dan: Yes please, before they sell out. I'll pay you back at the theater.
Priya: Got it. Let's meet at the popcorn stand at 8:45.
Dan: Perfect. See you there!

questions.q: Why do they switch to the 9:00 show?
questions.explanation: Dan says his meeting runs till 7, so he can't make the 7:30 showing.

questions.q: What does Dan say he will do at the theater?
options: Pay Priya back for his ticket
Wait for Priya at the entrance
Bring popcorn for both of them
questions.explanation: Priya buys the tickets, and Dan promises to pay her back when they meet at the theater.

title: Memo: shared office kitchen
passage:
MEMO
To: All Staff
From: Office Management
Re: Shared Kitchen

We've received several complaints about the state of the third-floor kitchen. Effective Monday, please rinse your dishes and load them into the dishwasher rather than leaving them in the sink. Any items left in the sink overnight will be placed in the 'lost and found' bin by the door.

The fridge will be cleaned out every Friday at 5 p.m. Unlabeled food will be thrown away, so mark your items with your name and the date.

Thank you for helping keep our space clean.

questions.q: What happens to dishes left in the sink overnight?
options: They are washed by the cleaning staff
They are placed in the lost and found bin
They are thrown away right away
They are returned to the owner's desk
questions.explanation: The memo states items left in the sink overnight go into the 'lost and found' bin by the door.

questions.q: How can employees keep their food from being thrown away on Friday?
options: Store it in a personal cooler
Label it with their name and the date
questions.explanation: Unlabeled food is discarded, so items must be marked with a name and date to be kept.

title: Notice: scheduled water shut-off
passage:
NOTICE TO ALL RESIDENTS

Scheduled Water Shut-Off
Building C — Tuesday, June 18

To repair a leaking main pipe, the water supply will be turned off from 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. Please store enough drinking water for the day and avoid running washing machines or dishwashers during this time.

Water may look cloudy or brown for a short while after service resumes. Let your cold tap run for two minutes to clear it.

We apologize for the inconvenience.
— Building Management

questions.q: Why will the water be turned off?
options: To repair a leaking main pipe
To clean the building's storage tanks
To test the fire sprinkler system
questions.explanation: The notice says the shut-off is needed to repair a leaking main pipe.

questions.q: What should residents do if the water looks brown after it comes back on?
options: Boil the water before drinking it
Run the cold tap for two minutes
Wait until the next morning to use it
questions.explanation: The notice advises running the cold tap for two minutes to clear the cloudy or brown water.

title: Social post: lost dog
passage:
Posted in Oakwood Neighbors group · 2h

LOST DOG — please share!
Our beagle, Cooper, slipped out of the yard this morning near Elm Park. He's brown and white and wears a red collar with no tag. He's friendly but shy around strangers, so please don't chase him—call or text me at 555-0177 if you spot him.

We miss him so much. Thank you, neighbors!

He is shy and could run away

He is not wearing a collar
The tag shows the wrong name
questions.explanation: The post notes the red collar has no tag, so it carries no identifying information.

title: Instructions: coffee maker quick start
passage:
QUICK-START GUIDE — AutoBrew 300

Before first use, wash the carafe and filter basket with warm, soapy water.

1. Fill the tank with cold water to the line for your desired number of cups.
2. Place a paper filter in the basket and add one scoop of ground coffee per cup.
3. Close the lid, set the carafe on the warming plate, and press START.
4. The green light turns off when brewing is complete.

Note: The warming plate shuts off automatically after 40 minutes.

questions.q: How much ground coffee should you add?
options: One scoop for the whole pot
questions.explanation: Step 2 says to add one scoop of ground coffee per cup.

questions.q: How do you know when brewing is finished?
options: The carafe fills to the line
questions.explanation: Step 4 says the green light turns off when brewing is complete.

LOST & FOUND — Riverside Public Library

Found this week:
• A pair of black gloves (children's section)
• A blue umbrella (front entrance)

Items are held at the circulation desk for 30 days. To claim yours, describe the item to a staff member; no receipt is needed. Unclaimed items are donated to a local charity at the end of each month.

Questions? Ask at the front desk or call 555-0143.

questions.q: How can someone show that a found item is theirs?

questions.explanation: The notice says to describe the item to a staff member, and that no receipt is needed.

They are sold at a library sale
They are donated to a local charity

title: Email: gym membership renewal
passage:
Subject: Your FitZone membership renews soon

Hi Marcus,

Your annual membership ends on August 31. It will renew automatically at $360 for the year unless you tell us otherwise. If you'd rather switch to monthly billing ($34/month), reply to this email before August 25.

As a renewing member, you'll keep your free locker and two guest passes per month. Want to pause instead? We can freeze your account for up to three months at no charge.

Thanks for training with us!
— The FitZone Team

questions.q: What will happen on August 31 if Marcus does nothing?
options: He will be moved to monthly billing
questions.explanation: The email says the annual membership renews automatically at $360 unless he responds.

questions.q: What benefit does the email say renewing members keep?
options: A free locker and two guest passes a month
questions.explanation: The email states renewing members keep their free locker and two guest passes per month.

title: Library overdue notice
passage:
OVERDUE NOTICE — Maple City Library

Account: T. Nguyen

The following item is 12 days overdue:
"World Atlas, 5th ed." — due May 3

A late fee of $0.25 per day is now $3.00. Fines stop growing once they reach $10.00 per item. Please return the book to any branch or the outdoor drop box.

If the book is lost, you will be billed its $40 replacement cost instead of the fine. Borrowing is blocked once fines pass $15 total.

They will owe a flat $10 fine
Their account will be closed permanently
questions.explanation: The notice says a lost book is billed at its $40 replacement cost instead of the daily fine.
